nodes.context: Mexican criminal organization and U.S.-designated Foreign Terrorist Organization that splintered from Los Zetas.
Mexican criminal organization from which Cártel del Noreste splintered.
Gang that controlled Nuevo Laredo and was later absorbed by the Gulf Cartel.
Mexican criminal organization that absorbed Los Texas and employed Miguel Ángel Treviño Morales.
Faction of Los Zetas aligned with the Gulf Cartel and rival to Cártel del Noreste.
Rival criminal organization listed in infobox.
Rival criminal organization listed in infobox, associated with Juaréz Cartel.
Rival criminal organization listed in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The Cártel del Noreste ... splintered from Los Zetas following the capture of their last absolute leader, Omar Treviño Morales.
edges.context: Cártel del Noreste is a splinter group of Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: In the latter, it is believed that they took away the cocaine purchasing business from Los Zetas, after their disappearance as a criminal group.
edges.context: Cártel del Noreste took over cocaine purchasing business from Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: The family had a relationship with the gang Los Texas (later absorbed by the Gulf Cartel in 2001), which controlled the city of Nuevo Laredo.
edges.context: Los Texas was absorbed by the Gulf Cartel in 2001.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: CJNG, Gulf Cartel, Zetas Vieja Escuela, La Línea (Juaréz Cartel), Sinaloa Cartel
edges.context: Zetas Vieja Escuela is listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: They are aligned with their original employers, the Gulf Cartel.
